topicQuestion: Go for higher pay at expense of social life (For) or take lower pay to enjoy life (Against)?
paraphrase: I agree that pay is important. But it is also important to manage our costs and expectations. Yes, with higher pay comes freedom, but it does not guarantee happiness. While low-middle incomed people do not have higher pay and may struggle to make ends meets, if they are still able to enjoy life, then it is still good. Lower pay does not necessarily equal to being poorer. And if you are in a position of being in need, then higher pay would make more sense, and you should focus your time to earn money so you could live to see tomorrow. But if you are already earning enough, then it’s time to enjoy life, there is no point in pursuing something that is never ending.

topicQuestion: Go for higher pay at expense of social life (For) or take lower pay to enjoy life (Against)?

At the end of the day i believe everyone has the choice to either make time, or choose jobs that can match the expectations of spending enough time at work and with the family
acknowledgement: at the end of the day, theres nothing inherently wrong with pursuing higher pay, as long as it isnt self-centered and at the expense of others, everyone has the choice on how they make time to pursuse happiness outside of work

topicQuestion: Go for higher pay at expense of social life (For) or take lower pay to enjoy life (Against)?
paraphrase: My opponent highlights why financial stability matters and the stress that financial insecurity can cause. However, it hasn’t significantly changed my position. If basic financial needs are met, having time for relationships and a healthy social life is just as important for long-term well-being, and sacrificing it for higher pay may not lead to a better quality of life.

topicQuestion: Go for higher pay at expense of social life (For) or take lower pay to enjoy life (Against)?
paraphrase: My opponent’s main point is that higher pay expands opportunities beyond basic needs. They argue that once necessities are covered, additional income allows people to pursue meaningful experiences such as travel. They also suggest that prioritising social life over financial security could backfire, leaving someone feeling financially constrained. However, many money-bought experiences require time and relationships to actually enjoy them. A job that pays more but limits social life may undermine happiness.

topicQuestion: Go for higher pay at expense of social life (For) or take lower pay to enjoy life (Against)?
paraphrase: My opponent’s point is that higher pay can actually help people manage stress. They acknowledge that high-paying jobs come with pressure, but argue that the additional resources like access to therapy, leisure activities, or better lifestyle options, can help people cope with stress. They also point out that financial insecurity creates its own persistent stress. I still believe that a lifestyle that protects time, relationships, and personal well-being alongside adequate income is more likely to produce lasting happiness than focusing on salary-maxxing.

topicQuestion: Go for higher pay at expense of social life (For) or take lower pay to enjoy life (Against)?
paraphrase: My opponent’s main point this round is that higher pay can create more flexibility in the future. They argue that earning more may give someone the financial cushion to explore different roles, seek jobs that align with their values. But the idea that more money automatically equals more freedom to change paths is a bit of a false choice. People who are not salary maxxing are not automatically financially trapped. Many still have the resources, support systems, and time to pursue new directions or further training.

topicQuestion: Go for higher pay at expense of social life (For) or take lower pay to enjoy life (Against)?
paraphrase: Your opponent’s main point this round is that higher pay provides a financial buffer, which supposedly makes it easier to take risks, develop new skills, and pursue different job opportunities. While higher pay can provide one form of buffer, it is not the only mechanism that enables people to grow, change jobs, or pursue meaningful opportunities.

topicQuestion: Should we use ChatGPT for homework?
paraphrase:
My opponent’s main concern was that allowing ChatGPT for homework could lead students to become overly dependent on AI, reducing genuine learning. They argued that if students rely on it to generate answers without engaging with the underlying concepts, they risk missing out on developing critical thinking and problem-solving skills.

After considering their point, I still believe the impact depends largely on how the tool is used. While the risk of misuse exists, I see it more as an issue of guidance and responsible integration rather than a reason for outright restriction.
